headline: EVOLUTION MINING HITS NET CASH: A$406M March Quarter Cash Flow Sends Shares 8.9% Higher in Sydney
snippet: Evolution Mining (ASX: EVN) posted A$406 million in group operating cash flow for the March 2026 quarter — A$2,482 per ounce — while achieving a net cash position of A$42 million for the first time, ending years of net debt. Gold production hit 170,000 ounces with copper adding 11,000 tonnes, and management revised full-year AISC guidance 6% lower to A$1,640–$1,760 per ounce.

headline: FIRST IN A DECADE: Burke Hollow Becomes First New U.S. ISR Uranium Mine as Spot U3O8 Climbs to $86/lb
snippet: Uranium Energy Corp's Burke Hollow facility in Texas commenced in-situ recovery operations, marking the first new ISR uranium mine in the United States in more than a decade. Spot U3O8 climbed to $86 per pound as fuel-cycle tightness, aggressive AI data centre contracting for small modular reactors, and fresh utility purchasing drove buying interest.

headline: BARRICK RECORD $7.69B FY2025 CASH FLOW, 25% DIVIDEND HIKE as Gold Miners Shake Off Q1 Rout
snippet: Barrick Mining posted record 2025 operating cash flow of $7.69 billion, a 71% year-over-year increase, and approved a 25% dividend increase under a new policy targeting 50% of attributable free cash flow. After shedding nearly 30% through March, Barrick shares rebounded over 15% in the first two weeks of April, with First Majestic Silver showing a similar reversal — analysts cite compressed multiples versus margins as the opportunity.

headline: MAKO MINING Q1 RECORD: 13,721 oz Au at $4,904 Average — Debt-Free With $96M Cash as Guyana Ramps
snippet: Mako Mining (TSX-V: MKO) reported record Q1 2026 gold sales of 13,721 ounces at an average realized price of $4,904 per ounce, generating $68.6 million in revenue. The company is now fully debt-free with $96.1 million in cash and receivables, while its Guyana Eagle Mountain project progressed its ESIA submission with construction to commence later this year.

headline: CHINA BANS SULFURIC ACID EXPORTS — And 70% of the World's Annual Silver Supply Hangs in the Balance
snippet: China announced a ban on sulfuric acid exports that analysts say threatens a critical input for copper heap-leach mining worldwide — and since approximately 70% of annual silver supply comes as a by-product of copper production, the move could materially tighten the physical silver market. Chile alone purchases around one million tonnes of acid from China annually to process its copper ore.

headline: HERAEUS WARNS: Monthly Bearish Engulfing on Gold and Silver Charts Could Signal Six-Month Bull Pause
snippet: Precious metals refiner Heraeus flagged bearish engulfing candlestick patterns on the March 2026 monthly charts for both gold and silver — a technical signal that last appeared in April 2022 and preceded a six-month consolidation. With gold near $4,810 and silver around $78, analysts say structural bull fundamentals remain intact but warn retail investors against chasing the recent move higher.

headline: MINEROS Q1 2026: 60,782 oz GEO (+10% YoY) as Nicaragua Ramps to 2,100 t/d — Upper Guidance in Sight
snippet: Mineros (TSX: MNSF) reported Q1 2026 gold equivalent production of 60,782 ounces, a 10% year-over-year increase, driven by a 22% production gain at its Hemco operation in Nicaragua after capacity expansions. Silver production surged 109% year-over-year to 161,766 ounces. The company is tracking toward the upper end of its 213,000–233,000 oz full-year guidance.

headline: SASKATCHEWAN MINING: $7 BILLION Invested in 2025 — Province Claims #3 Globally for Attractiveness
snippet: Saskatchewan's Mining Supply Chain Forum drew over 2,500 participants this week — a second consecutive attendance record — as the province reported an estimated $7 billion in mining investment for 2025 and a Fraser Institute ranking of third globally for investment attractiveness. With NexGen Energy and Denison Mines advancing major uranium projects, the Athabasca Basin is emerging as a global critical minerals hub.

headline: CHINA PAUSES Rare Earth Export Controls Through November — But the Supply Chain Leverage Hasn't Gone Away
snippet: China suspended implementation of rare earth export controls — covering samarium, terbium, dysprosium, and four other critical elements — until November 10, 2026, following diplomatic talks in Kuala Lumpur. Analysts warn the pause is strategic rather than structural: China still controls roughly 98% of heavy rare earth refining capacity, and approved export applications remain at Beijing's discretion, meaning supply chain risk has not been eliminated.

headline: LITHIUM CARBONATE +78% IN ONE MONTH: CATL Mine Suspension and China Permit Revocations Squeeze Supply
snippet: Lithium carbonate prices in China surged 78.3% in just over a month, reaching their highest level since late 2023, driven by the prolonged suspension of CATL's Jianxiawo mine and local authorities in Yichun canceling 27 lithium-related mining permits as part of Beijing's anti-overcapacity campaign. The China VAT export rebate cut on lithium-ion batteries from April 1, 2026 added further upward pressure on battery-grade material.
